title: Kennedy UNCF Feb 7
description:
ood Afternoon,

Following my phone conversation with Halley, I'm reaching out to secure your services for our upcoming event.

**Event Details**

- Event: 2nd Annual UNCF Triad "A Mind Is…" Reception (AMI)
- Date: Friday, February 7th at 6:00 PM
- Location: Sheraton Greensboro at the Four Seasons
- Setup Timeline: Ballroom available Friday at 12:00 PM and all day February 7th for preparation before 6:00 PM

‌

**Service Request: 2 Half Garlands with Marble Touches**

We need two (2) half garlands to border our 10x8 step-and-repeats. Please see the attached reference photo for the desired style.

- Design: Garlands extending across the top and down one side of both step-and-repeats
- Color Scheme: Gold and emerald green balloons with black & gold marble accents
- Note: We prefer marble touches instead of florals (as mentioned in our previous quote)

‌

**To move forward, please provide:**

- Signed W9 form
- Invoice with total amount
- Basic contract for both parties to sign

We'd prefer to provide a check on-site the day of the event. However, if that doesn't work for you, we can arrange an EFT deposit by next week. Please let me know your preference, and I'll get everything set up in our system.

We look forward to working with you all!

title: Booking Process
description:
**Client Follow-Up Process & Booking Checklist**

---

**🌟 Goal:** Convert inquiries into booked clients efficiently and warmly

---

### 📅 STEP 1: Send First Email (Personalized + Friendly)

**Timing:** Within 24 hours of form submission

- Use client name
- Link to decor guide or portfolio
- Invite them to book a design call or reply with ideas

---

### 💊 STEP 2: Send Follow-Up Text

**Timing:** Within 12 hours of sending the email

- Check if they saw your message
- Include link to schedule call or direct question like "Did anything catch your eye?"

---

### 📝 STEP 3: Try to Book Design Call

**Timing:** In the text or second email

- Offer specific times or a Calendly link
- Keep tone excited and collaborative ("Would love to brainstorm with you!")

---

### 📱 STEP 4: Make Phone Call

**Timing:** If no response after text/email

- Leave a voicemail if needed
- Mention you're just checking in and available to help brainstorm

---

### 🖋️ STEP 5: Send Proposal (if applicable)

**Timing:** Within 24 hours of call or once details confirmed

- Include pricing, visuals, and expiration date
- Mention custom bundles if helpful

---

### 🚫 STEP 6: Light Check-In Email/Text

**Timing:** 1–2 days after proposal

- Soft reminder tone: "Just checking in – any thoughts or questions?"
- Reassure there’s no pressure, happy to help tweak anything

---

### ❌ STEP 7: Final Follow-Up Email

**Timing:** 2–3 days after previous contact

- Give them a friendly heads-up you'll close the file if no response
- Invite them to reach out anytime if things change

---

title: Notes for proposals:
description:
Do not offer helium bouquets for outdoors, they dont do well and blow around.

‌

Switch up the pictures some, the lil meatball is not my fav to keep using.

‌

Offer starburst on alot of the designs, and if there is a theme offer matching mylars

‌

If there is a theme, get creative to play into that theme, even look online.

‌

Take some time to switch it up and offer new things when you can.

Hi [Client’s First Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out to us about your upcoming **[event type - baby shower, birthday, wedding, etc.]**! We’re so excited for you, and we love the theme and colors you’ve chosen - it’s going to look amazing.

Based on the details you shared in your booking form, I went ahead and created a **custom proposal** just for you. You’ll find it attached here: [Insert Link or PDF].

This is just a starting point to help you see how everything could come together.

If the design isn’t exactly what you had in mind - no worries at all! We can make adjustments, swap out décor, or even start fresh with a design call.

Our goal is to bring your exact vision to life, so we’re happy to tweak things until it feels perfect.

We truly look forward to working with you and helping make your [event type] one to remember. Once you’ve had a chance to look over the proposal, just let us know how you’d like to move forward.

Warmly,
Halley
Carolina Balloons

Hi [First Name]! This is Halley with Carolina Balloons 🎈 I just emailed you a custom proposal for your upcoming [event type]. Take a look when you get a chance—I’m happy to make any changes so it fits your exact vision!

title: Jasmine Mitchell 01/19/2026 [Google Ads]
description:
Google Ads:
Jasmine Mitchell
mitchell@firstcalvary.org
2022460165
My name is Rev. Jasmine Mitchell, and I serve as the Next Generation Director at First Calvary Baptist Church. We are currently preparing for our Annual Martin Luther King Jr. Family Serve Day, taking place on Monday, January 19, from 10:00 a.m. – 2:00 p.m.

We are seeking three (3) wide, high-impact balloon arches for this community-wide service event. Each arch will serve as a visual anchor in a high-traffic area and should feel energetic, dignified, and welcoming, appropriate for a celebratory yet meaningful MLK Day experience.

Arch 1 – Event Entrance (Arrival Experience)
This arch will be placed at the main entrance of the event space as participants arrive. A live drumline will be performing during arrival, so this arch should be wide, bold, and dynamic, creating excitement and setting the tone for the day.

Arch 2 – Auditorium Stage
This arch will be installed on the stage in the auditorium and should frame the space well for both in-person engagement and photography. It should feel elevated and polished without overpowering the stage or speakers.

Arch 3 – Gym / Service Area
This arch will be located in the gym where hands-on service projects will take place. It should be wide and welcoming, helping to define the service area and maintain energy throughout the day.

Design & Size Notes

All arches should be wide enough to fill large spaces and create strong visual impact

Preferred style: organic / modern balloon arches

Color palette should include black and gold, with openness to white or greenery for balance and inclusivity

Designs should photograph well and remain sturdy for the duration of the event

Please include recommended dimensions, pricing, setup requirements, and breakdown timing in your response.
1309 Halley St, Durham, NC 27707
01/19/2026
